The benchmark includes 3,400 document images across 17 languages, providing a comprehensive assessment of model capabilities. The findings indicate that while closed-source models perform better, open-source models struggle significantly, revealing the need for improvements in multilingual document parsing systems.

zh.title: 多语言文档解析的公平性与挑战
zh.desc: 我们介绍了多语言文档解析基准（MDPBench），这是第一个针对多语言数字和拍摄文档解析的基准。尽管文档解析在干净、数字化的页面上取得了显著进展，但在非拉丁文字和真实拍摄文档上，开源模型的表现明显不如闭源模型。MDPBench包含3400个文档图像，涵盖17种语言和多种脚本，提供高质量的标注。我们的评估结果显示，闭源模型在多种条件下表现相对稳健，而开源模型在非拉丁脚本和拍摄文档上则表现不佳，平均下降幅度达到17.8%。

papers.title: Gated Condition Injection without Multimodal Attention: Towards Controllable Linear-Attention Transformers
papers.abstract: Abstract Controllable diffusion models using linear attention architectures enable secure on-device visual generation with improved multi-condition input handling and faster convergence.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in diffusion-based controllable visual generation have led to remarkable improvements in image quality. However, these powerful models are typically deployed on cloud servers due to their large computational demands, raising serious concerns about user data privacy. To enable secure and efficient on-device generation, we explore in this paper controllable diffusion models built upon linear attention architectures, which offer superior scalability and efficiency, even on edge devices. Yet, our experiments reveal that existing controllable generation frameworks, such as ControlNet and OminiControl, either lack the flexibility to support multiple heterogeneous condition types or suffer from slow convergence on such linear-attention models. To address these limitations, we propose a novel controllable diffusion framework tailored for linear attention backbones like SANA. The core of our method lies in a unified gated conditioning module working in a dual-path pipeline, which effectively integrates multi-type conditional inputs, such as spatially aligned and non-aligned cues. Extensive experiments on multiple tasks and benchmarks demonstrate that our approach achieves state-of-the-art controllable generation performance based on linear-attention models, surpassing existing methods in terms of fidelity and controllability.

ru.title: Управляемая генерация на устройстве: диффузионные модели с линейным вниманием
ru.desc: В работе предлагается новый фреймворк для управляемой генерации изображений, основанный на диффузионных моделях с линейной архитектурой внимания. Основная проблема состояла в том, что существующие методы контроля, такие как ControlNet, либо не поддерживают множество разнородных типов условий, либо сходятся медленно на линейных моделях внимания. Авторы разработали унифицированный модуль условной обработки с механизмом gating, работающий в двухпотоковом конвейере и эффективно интегрирующий различные типы входных условий. Предложенный подход обеспечивает возможность безопасной генерации изображений прямо на устройстве пользователя с улучшенной точностью и степенью управляемости.

en.title: Empowering On-Device Visual Generation with Linear Attention
en.desc: This paper presents a new approach to controllable diffusion models that utilize linear attention architectures for on-device visual generation. The proposed method addresses the challenges of existing frameworks, which struggle with handling multiple types of input conditions and slow convergence rates. By introducing a unified gated conditioning module in a dual-path pipeline, the model effectively integrates various conditional inputs, enhancing both fidelity and controllability. Experimental results show that this novel framework outperforms current methods, making it a significant advancement in secure and efficient image generation on edge devices.

zh.title: 安全高效的设备端可控生成
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了一种基于线性注意力架构的可控扩散模型，旨在实现安全的设备端视觉生成。该模型能够更好地处理多种条件输入，并且收敛速度更快，适合在边缘设备上运行。我们提出了一种新颖的可控扩散框架，采用统一的门控条件模块，能够有效整合多类型的条件输入。实验结果表明，我们的方法在可控生成性能上达到了最先进的水平，超越了现有的技术。

papers.title: Omni123: Exploring 3D Native Foundation Models with Limited 3D Data by Unifying Text to 2D and 3D Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Omni123 is a 3D-native foundation model that unifies text-to-2D and text-to-3D generation using a shared sequence space with cross-modal consistency as an implicit structural constraint.  AI-generated summary Recent multimodal large language models have achieved strong performance in unified text and image understanding and generation, yet extending such native capability to 3D remains challenging due to limited data. Compared to abundant 2D imagery, high-quality 3D assets are scarce, making 3D synthesis under-constrained. Existing methods often rely on indirect pipelines that edit in 2D and lift results into 3D via optimization, sacrificing geometric consistency. We present Omni123, a 3D-native foundation model that unifies text-to-2D and text-to-3D generation within a single autoregressive framework. Our key insight is that cross-modal consistency between images and 3D can serve as an implicit structural constraint. By representing text, images, and 3D as discrete tokens in a shared sequence space, the model leverages abundant 2D data as a geometric prior to improve 3D representations. We introduce an interleaved X-to-X training paradigm that coordinates diverse cross-modal tasks over heterogeneous paired datasets without requiring fully aligned text-image-3D triplets. By traversing semantic-visual-geometric cycles (e.g., text to image to 3D to image) within autoregressive sequences, the model jointly enforces semantic alignment, appearance fidelity, and multi-view geometric consistency. Experiments show that Omni123 significantly improves text-guided 3D generation and editing, demonstrating a scalable path toward multimodal 3D world models.

ru.title: Единое пространство для 2D и 3D: кросс-модальная консистентность как геометрическое ограничение
ru.desc: Omni123 — это 3D-нативная фундаментальная модель, которая объединяет генерацию текста в 2D и текста в 3D в единой архитектуре авторегрессивного трансформатора. Ключевая идея заключается в использовании кросс-модальной консистентности между изображениями и 3D-объектами как неявного структурного ограничения. Модель представляет текст, изображения и 3D в виде дискретных токенов в общем пространстве последовательностей, позволяя использовать обильные 2D-данные как геометрический приор для улучшения 3D-представлений. Предложенная парадигма чередующегося обучения X-to-X координирует разнообразные кросс-модальные задачи через циклы "текст-изображение-3D-изображение", обеспечивая семантическую выравненность, верность внешнего вида и многовидовую геометрическую консистентность.

en.title: Unifying 2D and 3D Generation with Omni123
en.desc: Omni123 is a novel 3D-native foundation model designed to seamlessly generate both 2D and 3D content from text inputs. It addresses the challenge of limited high-quality 3D data by utilizing a shared sequence space that maintains cross-modal consistency between images and 3D representations. The model employs an innovative training approach that allows it to learn from diverse datasets without needing perfectly aligned text-image-3D pairs. By integrating semantic, visual, and geometric information, Omni123 enhances the quality of text-guided 3D generation and editing, paving the way for advanced multimodal 3D applications.

zh.title: Omni123：统一文本与3D生成的创新模型
zh.desc: Omni123是一个3D原生基础模型，能够统一文本到2D和文本到3D的生成。该模型通过共享的序列空间和跨模态一致性作为隐式结构约束，解决了3D生成中的数据稀缺问题。与传统方法不同，Omni123直接在一个自回归框架中处理多种模态，利用丰富的2D数据作为几何先验来改善3D表示。实验表明，Omni123在文本引导的3D生成和编辑方面显著提升了性能，展示了多模态3D世界模型的可扩展路径。

papers.title: Executing as You Generate: Hiding Execution Latency in LLM Code Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Parallel execution paradigm for LLM-based coding agents reduces latency by executing code during generation rather than in sequential stages.  AI-generated summary Current LLM-based coding agents follow a serial execution paradigm: the model first generates the complete code, then invokes an interpreter to execute it. This sequential workflow leaves the executor idle during generation and the generator idle during execution, resulting in unnecessary end-to-end latency. We observe that, unlike human developers, LLMs produce code tokens sequentially without revision, making it possible to execute code as it is being generated. We formalize this parallel execution paradigm, modeling it as a three-stage pipeline of generation, detection, and execution, and derive closed-form latency bounds that characterize its speedup potential and operating regimes. We then present Eager, a concrete implementation featuring AST-based chunking, dynamic batching with gated execution, and early error interruption. We evaluate Eager across four benchmarks, seven LLMs, and three execution environments. Results show that Eager reduces the non-overlapped execution latency by up to 99.9% and the end-to-end latency by up to 55% across seven LLMs and four benchmarks.

ru.title: Генерировать и выполнять одновременно: параллелизм для кодирующих агентов
ru.desc: В работе предложена новая парадигма параллельного выполнения для агентов, основанных на больших языковых моделях, которые пишут код. Вместо того чтобы сначала полностью генерировать код, а затем его выполнять, система Eager начинает выполнять код во время его генерации. Авторы формализуют этот подход как трёхэтапный конвейер: генерация, детектирование и выполнение. В результате удаётся сократить задержку выполнения на 99,9% и общую время обработки на 55% благодаря использованию синтаксических деревьев, динамического батчинга и ранней обработки ошибок.

en.title: Revolutionizing Code Execution: Parallel Processing for LLMs
en.desc: This paper introduces a new approach for large language model (LLM)-based coding agents that allows for parallel execution of code, which significantly reduces latency. Traditionally, these agents generate code in a sequential manner, leading to idle times during both code generation and execution. The authors propose a three-stage pipeline that includes generation, detection, and execution, enabling code to be executed as it is being generated. Their implementation, called Eager, demonstrates a remarkable reduction in execution latency by up to 99.9% and overall latency by up to 55% across various benchmarks and LLMs.

zh.title: 并行执行，提升编码效率！
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种基于大语言模型（LLM）的并行执行范式，用于编码代理，以减少延迟。传统的编码代理采用串行执行方式，先生成完整代码再执行，导致生成和执行阶段的空闲时间。研究表明，LLM可以在生成代码的同时进行执行，从而提高效率。论文中介绍了Eager的具体实现，展示了其在多个基准测试中的显著性能提升。

papers.title: LinguDistill: Recovering Linguistic Ability in Vision- Language Models via Selective Cross-Modal Distillation
papers.abstract: Abstract LinguDistill enables recovery of linguistic capabilities in vision-language models through adapter-free distillation using frozen language models as teachers, achieving performance close to pre-adaptation levels while maintaining visual task performance.  AI-generated summary Adapting pretrained language models (LMs) into vision-language models (VLMs) can degrade their native linguistic capability due to representation shift and cross-modal interference introduced during multimodal adaptation. Such loss is difficult to recover, even with targeted task-specific fine-tuning using standard objectives. Prior recovery approaches typically introduce additional modules that act as intermediate alignment layers to maintain or isolate modality-specific subspaces, which increases architectural complexity, adds parameters at inference time, and limits flexibility across models and settings. We propose LinguDistill, an adapter-free distillation method that restores linguistic capability by utilizing the original frozen LM as a teacher. We overcome the key challenge of enabling vision-conditioned teacher supervision by introducing layer-wise KV-cache sharing, which exposes the teacher to the student's multimodal representations without modifying the architecture of either model. We then selectively distill the teacher's strong linguistic signal on language-intensive data to recover language capability, while preserving the student's visual grounding on multimodal tasks. As a result, LinguDistill recovers sim10% of the performance lost on language and knowledge benchmarks, while maintaining comparable performance on vision-heavy tasks. Our findings demonstrate that linguistic capability can be recovered without additional modules, providing an efficient and practical solution to modality-specific degradation in multimodal models.
affiliations: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence

ru.title: Восстановление языковых навыков в мультимодальных моделях через эффективную дистилляцию
ru.desc: LinguDistill — это метод дистилляции знаний, который восстанавливает лингвистические способности видео-языковых моделей без добавления адаптеров. Авторы используют замороженную исходную языковую модель в качестве учителя и вводят послойное кэширование KV для передачи мультимодальных представлений от ученика к учителю. Метод селективно дистиллирует сильный лингвистический сигнал на языковоёмких данных, одновременно сохраняя визуальное понимание студента на мультимодальных задачах. В результате достигается восстановление примерно 10% потерянной производительности на языковых тестах без увеличения сложности архитектуры.

en.title: Restoring Language Skills in Vision-Language Models Efficiently
en.desc: LinguDistill is a method designed to restore the linguistic abilities of vision-language models (VLMs) that have been adapted from pretrained language models (LMs). When adapting these models, their language skills can suffer due to changes in representation and interference from visual data. LinguDistill addresses this issue by using the original frozen LM as a teacher, allowing it to guide the VLM without adding complexity to the model architecture. This approach successfully recovers about 10% of the lost linguistic performance while maintaining the model's effectiveness in visual tasks.

zh.title: 无适配器蒸馏，恢复语言能力！
zh.desc: LinguDistill是一种无适配器的蒸馏方法，旨在恢复视觉-语言模型中的语言能力。通过使用冻结的语言模型作为教师，LinguDistill能够在不增加额外模块的情况下，克服多模态适应带来的表示转移和跨模态干扰。该方法通过层级KV缓存共享，使教师能够接触学生的多模态表示，从而有效地提取语言信号。最终，LinguDistill在语言和知识基准上恢复了约10%的性能损失，同时在视觉任务上保持了相似的表现。

papers.title: UniRecGen: Unifying Multi-View 3D Reconstruction and Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract UniRecGen combines feed-forward reconstruction and diffusion-based generation in a shared canonical space to produce complete and consistent 3D models from sparse inputs through disentangled cooperative learning.  AI-generated summary Sparse-view 3D modeling represents a fundamental tension between reconstruction fidelity and generative plausibility. While feed-forward reconstruction excels in efficiency and input alignment, it often lacks the global priors needed for structural completeness. Conversely, diffusion-based generation provides rich geometric details but struggles with multi-view consistency. We present UniRecGen, a unified framework that integrates these two paradigms into a single cooperative system. To overcome inherent conflicts in coordinate spaces, 3D representations, and training objectives, we align both models within a shared canonical space. We employ disentangled cooperative learning, which maintains stable training while enabling seamless collaboration during inference. Specifically, the reconstruction module is adapted to provide canonical geometric anchors, while the diffusion generator leverages latent-augmented conditioning to refine and complete the geometric structure. Experimental results demonstrate that UniRecGen achieves superior fidelity and robustness, outperforming existing methods in creating complete and consistent 3D models from sparse observations.
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, Hong Kong
Macau University of Science and Technology, Macau

ru.title: Сотрудничество реконструкции и генерации для полных 3D моделей из разреженных данных
ru.desc: UniRecGen — это унифицированный подход для восстановления полных 3D моделей из разреженных изображений, который комбинирует два дополняющих друг друга метода: прямое восстановление и диффузионную генерацию. Оба модуля работают в общем каноническом пространстве, что позволяет преодолеть конфликты в координатных системах и целевых функциях обучения. Модуль восстановления обеспечивает геометрические опорные точки, а диффузионный генератор использует условную латентную аугментацию для уточнения и дополнения структуры. Разделенное кооперативное обучение стабилизирует тренировку и обеспечивает синергетическое взаимодействие моделей при инференсе, достигая превосходных результатов по полноте и консистентности восстановленных моделей.

en.title: Bridging Reconstruction and Generation for 3D Modeling
en.desc: UniRecGen is a novel framework that merges feed-forward reconstruction and diffusion-based generation to create complete 3D models from limited input data. It addresses the challenge of balancing reconstruction accuracy with generative detail by aligning both methods in a shared canonical space. The approach utilizes disentangled cooperative learning to ensure stable training and effective collaboration between the two models during the generation process. Experimental results show that UniRecGen significantly improves the quality and consistency of 3D models compared to previous techniques.

zh.title: 统一框架，生成完美3D模型
zh.desc: UniRecGen 是一个结合前馈重建和基于扩散生成的统一框架，旨在从稀疏输入中生成完整且一致的 3D 模型。该方法通过解耦合作学习，解决了重建精度与生成合理性之间的矛盾。前馈重建在效率和输入对齐方面表现优异，但缺乏全局先验信息，而扩散生成则提供丰富的几何细节，但在多视图一致性上存在挑战。实验结果表明，UniRecGen 在从稀疏观测中创建完整一致的 3D 模型方面，优于现有方法，展现了更高的保真度和鲁棒性。

papers.title: Memory-Augmented Vision-Language Agents for Persistent and Semantically Consistent Object Captioning
papers.abstract: Abstract A memory-augmented vision-language agent simultaneously handles data association, object captioning, and exploration policy within a single autoregressive framework, ensuring consistent object representation across viewpoints.  AI-generated summary Vision-Language Models (VLMs) often yield inconsistent descriptions of the same object across viewpoints, hindering the ability of embodied agents to construct consistent semantic representations over time. Previous methods resolved inconsistencies using offline multi-view aggregation or multi-stage pipelines that decouple exploration, data association, and caption learning, with limited capacity to reason over previously observed objects. In this paper, we introduce a unified, memory-augmented Vision-Language agent that simultaneously handles data association, object captioning, and exploration policy within a single autoregressive framework. The model processes the current RGB observation, a top-down explored map, and an object-level episodic memory serialized into object-level tokens, ensuring persistent object identity and semantic consistency across extended sequences. To train the model in a self-supervised manner, we collect a dataset in photorealistic 3D environments using a disagreement-based policy and a pseudo-captioning model that enforces consistency across multi-view caption histories. Extensive evaluation on a manually annotated object-level test set, demonstrate improvements of up to +11.86% in standard captioning scores and +7.39% in caption self-similarity over baseline models, while enabling scalable performance through a compact scene representation. Code, model weights, and data are available at https://hsp-iit.github.io/epos-vlm/.
affiliations: Italian Institute of Technology, Genoa, Italy

ru.title: Единая память для согласованного восприятия мира робота
ru.desc: В работе предложен единый агент на основе Vision-Language Models, дополненный модулем памяти, который одновременно решает задачи ассоциации объектов, генерации описаний и планирования исследования сцены в едином авторегрессивном фреймворке. Модель обрабатывает RGB-наблюдения, карту исследованной области и эпизодическую память объектов, представленную в виде токенов, что обеспечивает согласованность семантического представления объектов при наблюдении с разных точек зрения. Для обучения без учителя авторы собрали датасет в фотореалистичных 3D-окружениях, используя политику на основе разногласий и модель псевдо-аннотирования, которая принуждает согласованность описаний объектов при мультивью-наблюдении. Предложенный подход достигает улучшения на 11.86% в стандартных метриках описания объектов и 7.39% в консистентности описаний по сравнению с базовыми методами.

en.title: Unified Memory for Consistent Vision-Language Understanding
en.desc: This paper presents a memory-augmented Vision-Language agent that integrates data association, object captioning, and exploration policy into a single autoregressive framework. By processing RGB observations, explored maps, and episodic memory, the model maintains consistent object identities and semantic representations across different viewpoints. Unlike previous methods that relied on multi-stage pipelines, this approach allows for real-time reasoning over previously observed objects. The model shows significant improvements in captioning accuracy and self-similarity, demonstrating its effectiveness in creating coherent descriptions in complex environments.

zh.title: 统一的记忆增强视觉-语言代理
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种增强记忆的视觉-语言代理，能够在单一自回归框架内同时处理数据关联、物体描述和探索策略。这种模型通过处理当前的RGB观察、从上到下的探索地图以及序列化的物体级记忆，确保了物体在不同视角下的一致性和语义连贯性。与以往的方法相比，该模型在自监督学习中使用了一种基于不一致性的策略，收集了真实感3D环境的数据集，从而提高了多视角描述的一致性。实验结果表明，该模型在标准描述评分和描述自相似性上均有显著提升，展示了其在复杂场景表示中的可扩展性。

papers.title: Ask or Assume? Uncertainty-Aware Clarification-Seeking in Coding Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract A multi-agent system using uncertainty-aware design improves LLM agent performance on underspecified software development tasks by detecting ambiguity and proactively seeking clarification.  AI-generated summary As Large Language Model (LLM) agents are increasingly deployed in open-ended domains like software engineering, they frequently encounter underspecified instructions that lack crucial context. While human developers naturally resolve underspecification by asking clarifying questions, current agents are largely optimized for autonomous execution. In this work, we systematically evaluate the clarification-seeking abilities of LLM agents on an underspecified variant of SWE-bench Verified. We propose an uncertainty-aware multi-agent scaffold that explicitly decouples underspecification detection from code execution. Our results demonstrate that this multi-agent system using OpenHands + Claude Sonnet 4.5 achieves a 69.40% task resolve rate, significantly outperforming a standard single-agent setup (61.20%) and closing the performance gap with agents operating on fully specified instructions. Furthermore, we find that the multi-agent system exhibits well-calibrated uncertainty, conserving queries on simple tasks while proactively seeking information on more complex issues. These findings indicate that current models can be turned into proactive collaborators, where agents independently recognize when to ask questions to elicit missing information in real-world, underspecified tasks.
affiliations: Faculty of Computer Science, University of Vienna, Vienna, Austria
UniVie Doctoral School Computer Science, University of Vienna, Vienna, Austria

ru.title: Активные агенты: когда задать вопрос важнее, чем сразу действовать
ru.desc: В работе предлагается многоагентная система, которая использует осведомленность об неопределенности для улучшения производительности LLM-агентов при выполнении недоопределенных задач разработки программного обеспечения. Система явно разделяет обнаружение неоднозначности и выполнение кода, позволяя агентам активно запрашивать уточнения вместо автономного выполнения. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение процента решенных задач с 61.2% до 69.4% благодаря проактивному поиску недостающей информации. Предложенный подход демонстрирует хорошо откалиброванную неопределенность, что делает LLM-агентов более эффективными сотрудниками в реальных, плохо специфицированных задачах.

en.title: Empowering LLMs: Proactive Clarification in Software Development
en.desc: This paper presents a multi-agent system designed to enhance the performance of Large Language Model (LLM) agents in software development tasks that are not clearly defined. The system focuses on detecting ambiguity in instructions and encourages agents to ask clarifying questions, similar to how human developers operate. By separating the processes of identifying underspecification and executing code, the proposed approach allows for better handling of uncertain situations. The results show that this uncertainty-aware design significantly improves task resolution rates compared to traditional single-agent setups, making LLMs more effective collaborators in real-world scenarios.

zh.title: 主动澄清，提升智能体性能
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种多智能体系统，利用不确定性感知设计来提升大型语言模型（LLM）在不明确软件开发任务中的表现。该系统能够检测模糊性，并主动寻求澄清，从而解决人类开发者在面对不明确指令时的自然反应。通过对LLM代理在不明确任务上的澄清能力进行系统评估，我们发现该多智能体系统的任务解决率达到了69.40%，显著高于传统单一代理的61.20%。这些结果表明，当前模型可以转变为主动的协作伙伴，独立识别何时需要提问以获取缺失的信息。

papers.title: Working Notes on Late Interaction Dynamics: Analyzing Targeted Behaviors of Late Interaction Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Late Interaction retrieval models exhibit length bias in multi-vector scoring and efficient similarity exploitation through MaxSim operator, as demonstrated on NanoBEIR benchmark.  AI-generated summary While Late Interaction models exhibit strong retrieval performance, many of their underlying dynamics remain understudied, potentially hiding performance bottlenecks. In this work, we focus on two topics in Late Interaction retrieval: a length bias that arises when using multi-vector scoring, and the similarity distribution beyond the best scores pooled by the MaxSim operator. We analyze these behaviors for state-of-the-art models on the NanoBEIR benchmark. Results show that while the theoretical length bias of causal Late Interaction models holds in practice, bi-directional models can also suffer from it in extreme cases. We also note that no significant similarity trend lies beyond the top-1 document token, validating that the MaxSim operator efficiently exploits the token-level similarity scores.

ru.title: Разоблачение скрытых смещений в многовекторных моделях поиска
ru.desc: В статье исследуются модели Late Interaction для поиска информации, которые используют многовекторное представление документов. Авторы выявили систематическое смещение результатов в зависимости от длины документов при вычислении схожести между запросом и документом. Они также анализируют распределение значений схожести и показывают, что оператор MaxSim эффективно извлекает релевантную информацию только из лучшей совпадающей пары токенов. Исследование проведено на бенчмарке NanoBEIR для современных моделей извлечения информации.

en.title: Unraveling Length Bias in Late Interaction Retrieval Models
en.desc: This paper investigates Late Interaction retrieval models, which are known for their effective performance in information retrieval tasks. It highlights a specific issue called length bias that occurs when using multi-vector scoring, affecting the retrieval results. The study also examines the MaxSim operator, which efficiently utilizes similarity scores but shows that there is little significant information beyond the top score. The findings reveal that both causal and bi-directional Late Interaction models can experience length bias, particularly in extreme scenarios, while confirming the effectiveness of the MaxSim operator in capturing relevant similarities.

zh.title: 揭示晚期交互模型的长度偏差与相似性利用
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了晚期交互检索模型中的长度偏差和MaxSim操作符的相似性利用效率。我们发现，使用多向量评分时，模型在检索性能上存在长度偏差，尤其是在极端情况下双向模型也会受到影响。通过在NanoBEIR基准上分析这些现象，我们验证了MaxSim操作符在利用令牌级相似性分数方面的有效性。结果表明，除了最佳得分之外，未发现显著的相似性趋势。

papers.title: ActionParty: Multi-Subject Action Binding in Generative Video Games
papers.abstract: Abstract ActionParty enables multi-agent video generation by introducing subject state tokens that disentangle global video rendering from individual action control, achieving simultaneous control of up to seven players across diverse environments.  AI-generated summary Recent advances in video diffusion have enabled the development of "world models" capable of simulating interactive environments. However, these models are largely restricted to single-agent settings, failing to control multiple agents simultaneously in a scene. In this work, we tackle a fundamental issue of action binding in existing video diffusion models, which struggle to associate specific actions with their corresponding subjects. For this purpose, we propose ActionParty, an action controllable multi-subject world model for generative video games. It introduces subject state tokens, i.e. latent variables that persistently capture the state of each subject in the scene. By jointly modeling state tokens and video latents with a spatial biasing mechanism, we disentangle global video frame rendering from individual action-controlled subject updates. We evaluate ActionParty on the Melting Pot benchmark, demonstrating the first video world model capable of controlling up to seven players simultaneously across 46 diverse environments. Our results show significant improvements in action-following accuracy and identity consistency, while enabling robust autoregressive tracking of subjects through complex interactions.

ru.title: Независимое управление несколькими агентами в видео через токены состояния
ru.desc: ActionParty представляет новый подход к многоагентной генерации видео, решая проблему привязки действий в диффузионных моделях. Метод вводит subject state tokens — скрытые переменные, которые отслеживают состояние каждого агента независимо от других. Благодаря пространственному механизму смещения модель разделяет глобальный рендеринг видеокадров и управление отдельными действиями агентов. На бенчмарке Melting Pot система продемонстрировала способность одновременно контролировать до семи игроков в 46 различных окружениях.

en.title: Control Multiple Characters in Video with ActionParty!
en.desc: ActionParty is a novel approach in multi-agent video generation that allows for the simultaneous control of multiple characters in a scene. It introduces subject state tokens, which are special variables that keep track of each character's state, helping to separate the overall video rendering from the individual actions of each character. This method addresses the challenge of action binding in video diffusion models, which typically struggle to link specific actions to the correct characters. By using a spatial biasing mechanism, ActionParty enhances the accuracy of action-following and maintains consistency in character identity across various environments.

zh.title: 多智能体视频生成的革命性进展
zh.desc: ActionParty 是一种多智能体视频生成模型，通过引入主体状态标记，将全局视频渲染与个体动作控制分离，从而实现对多达七个玩家的同时控制。该模型解决了现有视频扩散模型在动作绑定方面的基本问题，能够将特定动作与相应主体关联。通过联合建模状态标记和视频潜变量，并采用空间偏置机制，ActionParty 提高了动作跟随的准确性和身份一致性。我们在 Melting Pot 基准测试中评估了 ActionParty，展示了其在 46 种不同环境中同时控制多个主体的能力。

papers.title: Automatic Image-Level Morphological Trait Annotation for Organismal Images
papers.abstract: Abstract Sparse autoencoders trained on foundation-model features produce monosemantic neurons that enable scalable extraction of morphological traits from biological images through a modular annotation pipeline.  AI-generated summary Morphological traits are physical characteristics of biological organisms that provide vital clues on how organisms interact with their environment. Yet extracting these traits remains a slow, expert-driven process, limiting their use in large-scale ecological studies. A major bottleneck is the absence of high-quality datasets linking biological images to trait-level annotations. In this work, we demonstrate that sparse autoencoders trained on foundation-model features yield monosemantic, spatially grounded neurons that consistently activate on meaningful morphological parts. Leveraging this property, we introduce a trait annotation pipeline that localizes salient regions and uses vision-language prompting to generate interpretable trait descriptions. Using this approach, we construct Bioscan-Traits, a dataset of 80K trait annotations spanning 19K insect images from BIOSCAN-5M. Human evaluation confirms the biological plausibility of the generated morphological descriptions. We assess design sensitivity through a comprehensive ablation study, systematically varying key design choices and measuring their impact on the quality of the resulting trait descriptions. By annotating traits with a modular pipeline rather than prohibitively expensive manual efforts, we offer a scalable way to inject biologically meaningful supervision into foundation models, enable large-scale morphological analyses, and bridge the gap between ecological relevance and machine-learning practicality.

ru.title: Автоматическое извлечение биологических признаков через интерпретируемые нейроны разреженных автокодировщиков
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод извлечения морфологических признаков из биологических изображений с использованием разреженных автокодировщиков, обученных на признаках фундаментальных моделей. Авторы продемонстрировали, что такие автокодировщики создают мономодальные нейроны, которые избирательно активируются на значимых морфологических структурах организмов. На основе этого свойства была разработана модульный конвейер аннотирования, использующий vision-language модели для генерации интерпретируемых описаний признаков. Создан датасет Bioscan-Traits из 80K аннотаций морфологических признаков, что позволяет масштабировать процесс анализа в экологических исследованиях и избежать дорогостоящей ручной разметки.

en.title: Scalable Trait Annotation for Ecological Insights
en.desc: This paper presents a method for extracting morphological traits from biological images using sparse autoencoders trained on foundation-model features. The authors demonstrate that these autoencoders produce monosemantic neurons that activate on specific morphological parts, allowing for effective localization and annotation of traits. They introduce a modular annotation pipeline that combines vision-language prompting to generate interpretable trait descriptions, resulting in the creation of the Bioscan-Traits dataset with 80,000 annotations from 19,000 insect images. This approach significantly reduces the reliance on manual annotation, making large-scale ecological studies more feasible and efficient.

zh.title: 高效提取生物形态特征的智能方法
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种稀疏自编码器，利用基础模型特征训练，能够提取生物图像中的形态特征。形态特征是生物体的物理特征，对理解生物与环境的互动至关重要。通过构建一个模块化的注释管道，我们能够快速定位重要区域并生成可解释的特征描述。最终，我们创建了一个包含8万条特征注释的Bioscan-Traits数据集，支持大规模生态研究。

papers.title: Woosh: A Sound Effects Foundation Model
papers.abstract: Abstract Woosh is a sound effect foundation model featuring audio encoding/decoding, text-audio alignment, and text-to-audio/video-to-audio generation capabilities with distilled versions for efficient deployment.  AI-generated summary The audio research community depends on open generative models as foundational tools for building novel approaches and establishing baselines. In this report, we present Woosh, Sony AI's publicly released sound effect foundation model, detailing its architecture, training process, and an evaluation against other popular open models. Being optimized for sound effects, we provide (1) a high-quality audio encoder/decoder model and (2) a text-audio alignment model for conditioning, together with (3) text-to-audio and (4) video-to-audio generative models. Distilled text-to-audio and video-to-audio models are also included in the release, allowing for low-resource operation and fast inference. Our evaluation on both public and private data shows competitive or better performance for each module when compared to existing open alternatives like StableAudio-Open and TangoFlux. Inference code and model weights are available at https://github.com/SonyResearch/Woosh. Demo samples can be found at https://sonyresearch.github.io/Woosh/.

ru.title: Универсальная модель для синтеза звуковых эффектов из текста и видео
ru.desc: Woosh — это большая языковая модель для звуковых эффектов от Sony AI, которая включает компоненты для кодирования и декодирования аудио, выравнивания текста и аудио. Модель обеспечивает генерацию аудио из текста и видео, а также предоставляет дистиллированные версии для эффективного развёртывания на устройствах с ограниченными ресурсами. Авторы провели детальную оценку архитектуры и процесса обучения, показав конкурентные или лучшие результаты по сравнению с открытыми альтернативами. Модель выпущена в открытом доступе с кодом и весами, что позволяет исследователям использовать её как основу для новых подходов в обработке звука.

en.title: Woosh: Revolutionizing Sound Effects with AI
en.desc: Woosh is a foundational model designed for generating sound effects, featuring advanced capabilities in audio encoding and decoding, as well as aligning text with audio. It includes models for generating audio from text and video, optimized for high-quality sound effects. The paper discusses the architecture and training of Woosh, comparing its performance to other open models like StableAudio-Open and TangoFlux. Additionally, distilled versions of the models are provided for efficient deployment in low-resource environments.

zh.title: Woosh：高效的声音效果生成模型
zh.desc: Woosh是一个声音效果基础模型，具备音频编码/解码、文本-音频对齐和文本到音频/视频到音频生成的能力。该模型经过优化，专注于声音效果，提供高质量的音频编码器/解码器和文本-音频对齐模型。我们还发布了经过蒸馏的文本到音频和视频到音频生成模型，以支持低资源操作和快速推理。我们的评估结果显示，Woosh在多个模块上与现有的开源模型相比，表现出竞争力或更好的性能。

papers.title: Apriel-Reasoner: RL Post-Training for General-Purpose and Efficient Reasoning
papers.abstract: Abstract Apriel-Reasoner is a 15B-parameter language model trained with reproducible multi-domain reinforcement learning to improve reasoning efficiency and accuracy across diverse tasks while reducing inference costs.  AI-generated summary Building general-purpose reasoning models using reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) across diverse domains has been widely adopted by frontier open-weight models. However, their training recipes and domain mixtures are often not disclosed. Joint optimization across domains poses significant challenges: domains vary widely in rollout length, problem difficulty and sample efficiency. Further, models with long chain-of-thought traces increase inference cost and latency, making efficiency critical for practical deployment. We present Apriel-Reasoner, trained with a fully reproducible multi-domain RL post-training recipe on Apriel-Base, a 15B-parameter open-weight LLM, across five domains using public datasets: mathematics, code generation, instruction following, logical puzzles and function calling. We introduce an adaptive domain sampling mechanism that preserves target domain ratios despite heterogeneous rollout dynamics, and a difficulty-aware extension of the standard length penalty that, with no additional training overhead, encourages longer reasoning for difficult problems and shorter traces for easy ones. Trained with a strict 16K-token output budget, Apriel-Reasoner generalizes to 32K tokens at inference and improves over Apriel-Base on AIME 2025, GPQA, MMLU-Pro, and LiveCodeBench while producing 30-50% shorter reasoning traces. It matches strong open-weight models of similar size at lower token cost, thereby pushing the Pareto frontier of accuracy versus token budget.

ru.title: Эффективное многодоменное рассуждение с адаптивной длиной цепочки мысли
ru.desc: Apriel-Reasoner — это языковая модель с 15 миллиардами параметров, обученная с помощью воспроизводимого многодоменного reinforcement learning для улучшения эффективности и точности рассуждений. Модель использует адаптивный механизм выборки доменов, который сбалансирует разнородные характеристики обучающих данных, и модифицированный штраф за длину цепочки рассуждений, учитывающий сложность задач. Несмотря на бюджет вывода всего в 16K токенов во время обучения, модель обобщается до 32K токенов при инференсе и достигает лучших результатов на тестах AIME 2025, GPQA и других, производя при этом на 30-50% более короткие цепочки рассуждений. Модель устанавливает новый паретов фронтир баланса между точностью и стоимостью токенов для open-weight моделей сопоставимого размера.

en.title: Efficient Reasoning Across Domains with Apriel-Reasoner
en.desc: Apriel-Reasoner is a large language model designed to enhance reasoning capabilities through multi-domain reinforcement learning. It utilizes a unique training approach that allows it to efficiently handle various tasks, such as mathematics and code generation, while minimizing inference costs. The model incorporates an adaptive domain sampling method to maintain balance across different task difficulties and a length penalty that adjusts based on problem complexity. As a result, Apriel-Reasoner achieves improved performance on several benchmarks while producing shorter reasoning outputs compared to its predecessor, Apriel-Base.

zh.title: 提升推理效率与准确性的多领域模型
zh.desc: Apriel-Reasoner 是一个拥有 150 亿参数的语言模型，采用可重复的多领域强化学习进行训练，以提高推理效率和准确性，同时降低推理成本。该模型在五个领域（数学、代码生成、指令跟随、逻辑难题和函数调用）上使用公共数据集进行训练。我们引入了一种自适应领域采样机制，能够在不同的领域动态中保持目标领域比例，并且采用了一种难度感知的标准长度惩罚扩展，鼓励在困难问题上进行更长的推理。最终，Apriel-Reasoner 在推理时能够扩展到 32K 令牌，并在多个基准测试中超越了 Apriel-Base，同时生成的推理轨迹缩短了 30-50%。

papers.title: NearID: Identity Representation Learning via Near-identity Distractors
papers.abstract: Abstract Researchers developed a novel framework using Near-identity distractors to improve identity-focused vision tasks by creating a dataset and evaluation protocol that better isolates identity from background context, leading to more reliable representations and metrics.  AI-generated summary When evaluating identity-focused tasks such as personalized generation and image editing, existing vision encoders entangle object identity with background context, leading to unreliable representations and metrics. We introduce the first principled framework to address this vulnerability using Near-identity (NearID) distractors, where semantically similar but distinct instances are placed on the exact same background as a reference image, eliminating contextual shortcuts and isolating identity as the sole discriminative signal. Based on this principle, we present the NearID dataset (19K identities, 316K matched-context distractors) together with a strict margin-based evaluation protocol. Under this setting, pre-trained encoders perform poorly, achieving Sample Success Rates (SSR), a strict margin-based identity discrimination metric, as low as 30.7% and often ranking distractors above true cross-view matches. We address this by learning identity-aware representations on a frozen backbone using a two-tier contrastive objective enforcing the hierarchy: same identity > NearID distractor > random negative. This improves SSR to 99.2%, enhances part-level discrimination by 28.0%, and yields stronger alignment with human judgments on DreamBench++, a human-aligned benchmark for personalization. Project page: https://gorluxor.github.io/NearID/
affiliations: King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST), Saudi Arabia
Snap Research, Palo Alto, CA, USA

ru.title: Изолирование идентичности от контекста через Near-identity дистракторы
ru.desc: Исследователи разработали новый фреймворк с использованием Near-identity дистракторов для улучшения задач, ориентированных на идентификацию в компьютерном зрении. Они создали датасет с семантически похожими, но различными экземплярами на одинаковом фоне, что изолирует идентичность как единственный дискриминативный сигнал, исключая влияние контекста. На основе этого принципа была предложена строгая метрика оценки Sample Success Rate и двухуровневая контрастивная функция потерь, обучаемая на замороженной архитектуре кодировщика. Предложенный подход значительно улучшил производительность с 30.7% до 99.2% и обеспечил лучшее соответствие с оценками человека на задачах персонализации.

en.title: Isolating Identity for Better Vision Tasks
en.desc: This paper presents a new framework that uses Near-identity distractors to enhance identity-focused vision tasks, such as personalized image generation and editing. By creating a dataset that isolates identity from background context, the researchers aim to improve the reliability of representations and evaluation metrics. The NearID dataset includes 19,000 identities and 316,000 matched-context distractors, which help to eliminate contextual shortcuts in identity recognition. The proposed method significantly boosts performance, achieving a Sample Success Rate of 99.2% and better alignment with human judgments.

zh.title: 用近似身份干扰物提升视觉任务的身份识别
zh.desc: 研究人员开发了一种新框架，使用近似身份干扰物（NearID distractors）来改善以身份为中心的视觉任务。该框架通过创建一个数据集和评估协议，更好地将身份与背景上下文分离，从而提高了表示和度量的可靠性。我们提出的NearID数据集包含19,000个身份和316,000个匹配上下文的干扰物，采用严格的边际评估协议。通过这种方法，预训练编码器的身份识别成功率显著提高，达到了99.2%。

papers.title: Friends and Grandmothers in Silico: Localizing Entity Cells in Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Entity-centric factual question answering involves localized MLP neurons that can be causally intervened to recover entity-consistent predictions, showing robustness to various linguistic variations but with limited universality across all entities.  AI-generated summary Language models can answer many entity-centric factual questions, but it remains unclear which internal mechanisms are involved in this process. We study this question across multiple language models. We localize entity-selective MLP neurons using templated prompts about each entity, and then validate them with causal interventions on PopQA-based QA examples. On a curated set of 200 entities drawn from PopQA, localized neurons concentrate in early layers. Negative ablation produces entity-specific amnesia, while controlled injection at a placeholder token improves answer retrieval relative to mean-entity and wrong-cell controls. For many entities, activating a single localized neuron is sufficient to recover entity-consistent predictions once the context is initialized, consistent with compact entity retrieval rather than purely gradual enrichment across depth. Robustness to aliases, acronyms, misspellings, and multilingual forms supports a canonicalization interpretation. The effect is strong but not universal: not every entity admits a reliable single-neuron handle, and coverage is higher for popular entities. Overall, these results identify sparse, causally actionable access points for analyzing and modulating entity-conditioned factual behavior.

ru.title: Скрытые нейроны языковых моделей раскрывают архив фактов об объектах
ru.desc: В работе исследуются внутренние механизмы языковых моделей, которые отвечают на факт-ориентированные вопросы об объектах. Авторы локализуют специализированные нейроны MLP, избирательно реагирующие на конкретные объекты, используя шаблонные промпты и причинно-следственные интервенции. Показано, что активация одного локализованного нейрона часто достаточна для восстановления согласованных с объектом предсказаний, что свидетельствует о компактном представлении информации об объектах в ранних слоях сети. Механизм демонстрирует устойчивость к различным вариантам написания, но охват не универсален и зависит от популярности объекта.

en.title: Unlocking Entity Knowledge with Targeted Neuron Activation
en.desc: This paper investigates how language models answer entity-centric factual questions by focusing on specific neurons in their architecture. The authors identify localized MLP neurons that can be manipulated to enhance predictions related to specific entities, demonstrating that these neurons are primarily found in the early layers of the model. Through experiments, they show that activating a single neuron can effectively retrieve accurate answers, although this method is not universally applicable to all entities. The findings suggest that while the model is robust to variations in language, its ability to consistently access entity information depends on the popularity of the entities involved.

zh.title: 通过局部神经元实现实体一致的问答
zh.desc: 这篇论文研究了实体中心的事实问答，特别是如何通过局部的多层感知器（MLP）神经元来实现一致的实体预测。研究表明，这些神经元在早期层集中，并且通过因果干预可以验证其有效性。激活单个局部神经元能够恢复与实体一致的预测，显示出对多种语言变体的鲁棒性。尽管这种方法在流行实体上效果更好，但并非所有实体都能可靠地通过单个神经元进行处理。

papers.title: Brainstacks: Cross-Domain Cognitive Capabilities via Frozen MoE-LoRA Stacks for Continual LLM Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract Brainstacks enables continual multi-domain fine-tuning of large language models through modular adapter stacks with MoE-LoRA, residual boosting, and outcome-based routing that discovers transferable cognitive primitives.  AI-generated summary We present Brainstacks, a modular architecture for continual multi-domain fine-tuning of large language models that packages domain expertise as frozen adapter stacks composing additively on a shared frozen base at inference. Five interlocking components: (1) MoE-LoRA with Shazeer-style noisy top-2 routing across all seven transformer projections under QLoRA 4-bit quantization with rsLoRA scaling; (2) an inner loop performing residual boosting by freezing trained stacks and adding new ones; (3) an outer loop training sequential domain-specific stacks with curriculum-ordered dependencies; (4) null-space projection via randomized SVD constraining new stacks to subspaces orthogonal to prior directions, achieving zero forgetting in isolation; (5) an outcome-based sigmoid meta-router trained on empirically discovered domain-combination targets that selectively weights stacks, enabling cross-domain composition. Two boundary experiments: (6) PSN pretraining on a randomly initialized model; (7) per-domain RL (DPO/GRPO) validating compatibility with post-SFT alignment. Validated on TinyLlama-1.1B (4 domains, 9 stacks) and Gemma 3 12B IT (5 domains, 10 stacks), MoE-LoRA achieves 2.5x faster convergence than parameter-matched single LoRA, residual boosting breaks through the single-stack ceiling, and the routed system recovers generation quality destroyed by ungated stack accumulation. The central finding: the outcome-based router discovers that domain stacks encode transferable cognitive primitives (instruction-following clarity, numerical reasoning, procedural logic, chain-of-thought structure) rather than domain-specific knowledge, with medical prompts routing to chat+math stacks in 97% of cases despite zero medical data in those stacks.

ru.title: Модульные стеки адаптеров для передачи когнитивных примитивов между доменами
ru.desc: Brainstacks представляет модульную архитектуру для непрерывного обучения больших языковых моделей на нескольких доменах с использованием адаптивных стеков с механизмом смешивания экспертов (MoE-LoRA) и QLoRA квантизацией. Система использует остаточное усиление путём последовательного добавления новых обученных стеков к замороженным предыдущим, а также проекцию в нулевое пространство для предотвращения катастрофического забывания. Основной компонент - маршрутизатор на основе исходов, который автоматически комбинирует стеки для решения задач, обнаруживая, что различные адаптеры кодируют общие когнитивные примитивы, такие как логическое рассуждение и структурирование мыслей. Экспериментально показано, что система достигает 2.5-кратного ускорения сходимости по сравнению с одиночным LoRA и успешно переносит компетенции между доменами.

en.title: Unlocking Transferable Skills in Language Models with Brainstacks
en.desc: Brainstacks is a novel architecture designed for the continual fine-tuning of large language models across multiple domains. It utilizes modular adapter stacks that allow for the integration of domain expertise while maintaining a shared base model. Key components include MoE-LoRA for efficient routing and residual boosting to enhance learning without forgetting previous knowledge. The system demonstrates that domain stacks can capture transferable cognitive skills, enabling effective cross-domain performance even with minimal domain-specific training data.

zh.title: Brainstacks：跨领域知识的智能组合
zh.desc: Brainstacks 是一种模块化架构，旨在通过适配器堆的持续多领域微调来提升大型语言模型的性能。它结合了 MoE-LoRA、残差增强和基于结果的路由，能够发现可转移的认知原语。该系统通过冻结训练好的适配器堆并添加新的堆来实现零遗忘，确保在不同领域之间的知识共享。实验结果表明，Brainstacks 在多个领域的任务中表现出色，能够有效地组合不同领域的知识。
